chapter_title: Animal, vegetable or microbial fats and oils and their cleavage products; prepared edible fats; animal or vegetable waxes
inclusions: Animal, vegetable or microbial fats and oils and their cleavage products
Margarine; edible mixtures or preparations of animal, vegetable or microbial fats or oils
Glycerol, crude; glycerol waters and glycerol lyes
Degras; residues resulting from the treatment of fatty substances or animal or vegetable waxes
Soap-stocks, oil foots and dregs, stearin pitch, glycerol pitch and wool grease residues (heading 15.22)
Virgin olive oil with free acidity expressed as oleic acid not exceeding 2.0 g/100 g (subheading 1509.30)
Low erucic acid rape or colza oil (erucic acid content of less than 2% by weight) (subheadings 1514.11 and 1514.19)

exclusions.condition: Pig fat or poultry fat of heading 02.09
Edible preparations containing by weight more than 15% of the products of heading 04.05 (butter/dairy fats)
Greaves or residues of headings 23.04 to 23.06
Fatty acids, prepared waxes, medicaments, paints, varnishes, soap, perfumery, cosmetic or toilet preparations, sulphonated oils or other goods of Section VI
Oils obtained from olives by solvent extraction
Fats or oils or their fractions, merely denatured
exclusions.action: Classify in the heading appropriate to the corresponding undenatured fats and oils and their fractions (Not 15.18)

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.15 (animal/vegetable fats and oils) vs Ch.33 (essential oils) vs Ch.34 (soap/lubricants)
classification_rules.description: Chapter 15 = fixed fats and oils (fatty acids/glycerides) for food or industrial use. Ch.33 = volatile essential oils (aromatic/perfumery, e.g., olive essential oil for fragrance). Ch.34 = soaps, lubricating preparations, waxes. The fixed vs. volatile nature, and degree of processing, separate these chapters.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: edible vs industrial fats/oils within Ch.15
classification_rules.description: Both edible (olive oil, palm oil, soybean oil) and industrial fats (technical-grade tallow, linseed oil for paint) classify in Ch.15 headings. The chapter does not split by food vs. non-food use; classification is by source/type of fat/oil. The specific heading (15.07 soybean, 15.09 olive, 15.11 palm, etc.) is determined by botanical/animal source.

classification_rules.rule: Crude vs refined oils within Ch.15
classification_rules.description: Crude vegetable oils (directly expressed or extracted, not purified) → specific oil heading (e.g., 15.07–15.15). Refined, bleached or deodorised → same specific oil heading. Hydrogenated, interesterified or otherwise chemically modified fats → 15.16. Margarine → 15.17. The degree of chemical modification determines the sub-heading within Ch.15.
